items.title: JPMorgan's Dimon: Credit card cap would lead to 'economic disaster'
items.description: One moment that stood out in JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon's interview at the World Economic Forum (WEF) in Davos, Switzerland, on Wednesday was when he referred to himself as a "globalist," making light of President Trump's own comments calling the big bank executive "a highly overrated globalist. Yahoo Finance Senior Bank Reporter David Hollerith highlights the top takeaways from Dimon's remarks, sharing his thoughts on the Trump administration's policy proposals on credit card rate caps and the broader banking industry. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

items.title: Is JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Still Attractively Priced After Recent Share Price Pullback
items.description: If you are wondering whether JPMorgan Chase is priced for its reputation or still offers room for value, it starts with understanding how the current share price lines up against the bank's fundamentals. The stock last closed at US$302.74, after a 2.6% decline over the past week and a 4.6% decline over the past month, while still showing a 17.5% return over 1 year and very large gains over 3 and 5 years.
